title: Chapter Checkpoint
brief: Make a tiny Python test pass by editing one function.
objectives: Run python3 test_app.py in the mission workspace.
hints: Open workspace/test_app.py too and read the exact expected string.
In app.py, make format_status return name + ': READY!' (uppercase READY and exclamation mark).
Run (from workspace/) python3 test_app.py, then run ./stage check.
primary_path: Best compare flow: `:vsplit workspace/test_app.py`, move between windows with `<C-w>w`.
Fix return string to `: READY!`, save `:w`, run `(from workspace/) python3 test_app.py`, then `./stage check`.
fallback_path: Buffer fallback: open test file once and use `:b#` to jump back and forth.
Tab fallback: `:tabedit workspace/test_app.py`, then `gt`/`gT` to switch tabs.
Use shell pane to rerun `python3 test_app.py` after every edit.
why_path: The test file defines exact output; checking it first removes guesswork and speeds up the fix loop.
tmux_reference: rerun command in shell pane: `python3 test_app.py`
pro_move: Keep one pane for editor and one pane for test reruns.
